skill.name: context7-integration
skill.description: Guides integration of Context7 for document ingestion, semantic search, scoped retrieval, and AI prompt context in web applications.
skill.summary: Integrate Context7 as a document-grounded knowledge layer with role and tenant-aware retrieval.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many alerts, but the weak cryptography, obfuscation, dynamic import, and backtick findings are false positives from YAML, Markdown, regex validation, and fenced examples. The skill has legitimate medium risk because it documents external API calls, environment credential use, recursive file ingestion, and tenant or role scoped retrieval that must be implemented carefully.

medium_findings.title: Document Content Can Be Sent to External Context7 API
medium_findings.description: The examples read local documents and send their content to a Context7 API using an environment API key. This is expected behavior for the integration, but it can expose sensitive documents if users ingest unreviewed files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code reads files, builds document payloads, and authenticates to the configured Context7 endpoint. The behavior is legitimate for this skill but has clear data exposure impact.

medium_findings.title: Tenant Scope Can Be Supplied by Ingestion Request
medium_findings.description: The ingestion request accepts school_id from the caller and uses it before falling back to current_user.school_id. If content-manager users are not globally trusted, this can allow cross-tenant document writes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The request model includes school_id and the handler uses request.school_id when present. The final impact depends on the application role model.

medium_findings.title: Broad Document Listing Before Tenant Filtering
medium_findings.description: The modules endpoint lists documents without passing tenant or role filters, then filters metadata in application code. This can expose cross-tenant metadata to the service layer and may leak module names.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The handler calls list_documents with only a limit before checking school_id locally. It returns only modules, so the issue is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives. The cited verify.py lines use regular expressions for frontmatter validation, and the cited SKILL.md lines are metadata, Markdown paths, or prose, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited script uses re.search and re.match to parse YAML fields. No hashing, encryption, or credential handling is present in those lines.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command and dynamic import alerts are largely false positives from Markdown backticks, file tree examples, imports in sample code, and integration tables. They do not execute when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence appears inside Markdown documentation blocks. The skill repository contains only SKILL.md and verify.py, so these snippets are guidance, not executable package code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token From Environment
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample client reads CONTEXT7_API_KEY and sends it as an Authorization bearer token to the configured Context7 base URL. This is normal API client behavior but should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential source and Authorization header assignment are directly visible in the cited lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Markdown File Ingestion
dangerous_patterns.description: The batch ingestion example recursively reads Markdown files from a user-provided directory and ingests them. Users must review directories before running it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example iterates over *.md files and sends each file through ingest_from_file. This is intentional but sensitive.

content.value_statement: Teams need grounded AI answers from internal documents without exposing every source to every user. This skill shows how to ingest documents, search them semantically, and apply role and tenant filters.
actual_capabilities: Defines a Context7 client for document ingestion, batch ingestion, search, retrieval, deletion, and namespace statistics.
Shows metadata models for document type, source tracking, role access, tenant scope, modules, and categories.
Provides FastAPI route examples for ingesting documents, searching knowledge, and listing available modules.
Includes a React hook pattern for calling a knowledge search endpoint and displaying results.
Documents context shaping utilities for prompt-ready excerpts with token limits and source attribution.
Includes a batch ingestion script pattern for Markdown document directories.
limitations: The skill is documentation and sample code, not a packaged Context7 SDK.
The examples require a real Context7 endpoint, API key, and application authentication layer.
Access control depends on the host application enforcing trusted roles and tenant boundaries.
File ingestion can expose sensitive documents if users run it on unreviewed directories.

use_cases.title: Build ERP Knowledge Search
use_cases.description: Add semantic search over policies, procedures, FAQs, and handbooks with module and tenant filters.

use_cases.title: Ground AI Chat Responses
use_cases.description: Retrieve relevant document chunks and format them as prompt context with source attribution.

use_cases.title: Prepare Controlled Document Ingestion
use_cases.description: Organize documents with metadata for roles, schools, modules, categories, and source tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a basic Context7 client for ingesting one document and searching it by query.

prompt_templates.title: Add Role Scoped Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a Context7 search flow that filters results by user role and school identifier.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Batch Ingestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a safe batch ingestion process for policy documents with metadata and review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Context7 Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Context7 integration for tenant isolation, credential handling, and prompt context quality.

output_examples.input: Create a Context7 ingestion plan for school policy documents.
output_examples.output: A staged ingestion plan with document folders, required metadata, role access rules, tenant scope, and validation checks before upload.

output_examples.input: Design a search endpoint that returns grounded context for chat.
output_examples.output: An endpoint design that authenticates the user, applies role and school filters, retrieves relevant chunks, and returns attributed context.

output_examples.input: Audit my Context7 usage for data exposure risks.
output_examples.output: A risk review covering API credentials, external transfer, file selection, role filters, tenant filters, and safe logging practices.

best_practices: Review documents for sensitive personal data before ingestion.
Enforce tenant and role filters on the server before any document data is returned.
Store Context7 credentials in managed secrets and restrict the base URL to approved endpoints.
anti_patterns: Do not ingest whole directories without reviewing file contents first.
Do not trust request-supplied tenant identifiers for non-administrator users.
Do not return raw retrieved context to users without source checks and length limits.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps developers integrate Context7 for document ingestion, semantic search, and scoped AI context retrieval.

faq.question: Does it include a working Context7 service?
faq.answer: No. It provides integration guidance and sample patterns for applications that already use or plan to use Context7.

faq.question: Why does it need an API key?
faq.answer: The sample client uses an API key to authenticate requests to the configured Context7 service endpoint.

faq.question: Can it handle multi-tenant applications?
faq.answer: Yes, the examples include school_id filters, but the host application must enforce tenant rules on the server.

faq.question: Is it safe to ingest private documents?
faq.answer: Only after review. Ingestion sends document content to the configured Context7 endpoint, so sensitive data controls are required.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
